category_title: Students with internalizing behaviors and low self-confidence
minimal_context: The student is prompted to join peers in playing a board game focused on moving around the alphabet based on animal names.
dialogue:
TUTOR: We are going to play a group board game now where we move around the alphabet based on animal names.

STUDENT: (Hesitates a bit) I don't want to play.

TUTOR: Please come over to the board and join the group.

STUDENT: (Shakes head and steps back) No.

TUTOR: It is time to start the game with everyone.

STUDENT: (Remains completely unwilling to participate in the group game, withdrawing from the others) I'm not doing it.

options.A: Immediately remove all demands and the board game materials for 60 seconds.
options.B: Allow the student to select their own group members for the board game.
options.C: Provide considerable encouragement to the student to join the group board game.
options.D: Engage in parallel play, acting silly and animated while singing, following the student's lead.
